# CAT-G-007 — NZIAT Artificial Intelligence Research Platform

| Field | Value |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Funder** | MBIE / New Zealand Institute for Advanced Technology |
| **Status** | **Watch / partner** — shortlisting done; full funding from **July 2026** |
| **Scale** | Up to **~$70 million over 7 years** (platform) |
| **URL** | https://www.mbie.govt.nz/science-and-technology/science-and-innovation/refocusing-the-science-innovation-and-technology-system/public-research-organisations/new-zealand-institute-for-advanced-technology/call-for-proposals-for-the-artificial-intelligence-research-platform |
| **Fit score** | **70** as industry / edge partner (not platform host) |

## Context

National AI research–industry platform. Shortlisted concepts (e.g. BioAI, Outdoor AI) emphasise real-world challenges (labour, pests, climate, exports) and **Māori co-governance / data sovereignty** in some concepts.

## CAT strategy

Do **not** wait to “be the platform.” Position Coastal Alpine as:

- Edge deployment partner (RPi + Hailo fleets)  
- Agritech / biosecurity pilot host  
- Sovereign offline inference reference architecture  

Engage shortlisted university/CRI leads (e.g. Waikato / Canterbury Outdoor AI narratives in public coverage).

## Next steps

1. Map shortlisted concepts to CAT portals  
2. Prepare one-pager: sovereign edge contribution  
3. Seek formal partner letter once platform host announced
